desc:
The road makes a turn to the north here. The outer portion of the wall makes a
grand sweeping curve allowing easier travel for wide loads. The inner portion of
the wall comes to a right angle.
name: Dwarven Road

desc:
The walls of the road here are covered with a fine green moss. The walls are
slightly damp but there is no available water. The moss only reaches down about
half of the wall and the floor is completely free of it. The road continues to
the east and west.
name: Dwarven Road

desc:
The faint smell of woodsmoke drifts in from the west. The walls accompanying the
road are bereft of any adornment. Looking in either direction the road continues
almost unerringly straight.
name: On a Dwarven Road

desc:
A small air vent blows a fresh breeze through the area. The air vent is high on
the wall and covered with a thick iron grate. The air is much warmer and is
tinged with a slight taint of wood smoke. The grate is the only blemish on an
otherwise smooth wall. The road continues to the east and west.
name: On a Dwarven Road

desc:
The soft drip of falling water can be heard falling somewhere off in the
distance. The acoustics are tricky and it is tough to tell whence the sound
came. The road continues to the east and west.
name: Dwarven Road

desc:
A large doorway breaks off from the road here. The doors are missing and the
hinges are nothing more than twisted metal slowly rusting away. The road
continues to the east and west oblivious to the flaw to the north.
name: Dwarven Road

desc:
The walls still contain a fiery vein of gold mixed with quartz. The thread wends
its way spiderlike through the granite. Whether the effect is in intentional or
something prevented the dwarves from mining this vein is unclear.
name: Golden Road

desc:
Occasionally, the vein of gold threads its way to the surface of the walls.
Seeming to trace in a spidery hand magical runes which entice the beholder. The
road continues to the north and east.
name: Golden Road

desc:
The gold vein makes a turn to the south. The light catching the gold creating
the image of fire alive in the walls. The tunnel widens a bit here as it makes
the turn.
name: Golden Road

desc:
The Road has been tunneled out from a Gold vein. The Dwarves have left some ore
in the tunnel. The precious metal is shimmering in the walls, it actually looks
like small pieces of light that just refuses to be swallowed by the overwhelming
darkness that meets your eye everywhere.
name: The Golden Road

desc:
The walls of this small tunnel are covered in a dull black matte finish. The
black coating extends to the north but curiously and abruptly ends as the road
continues to the south.
name: Dark Road

desc:
The hall is square with a raised platform at the northern end, and the floor is
covered with granite tiles. Many of the tiles are cracked after years of
neglect, and all are dull and scuffed.
name: Mustering Hall

desc:
This is the northwest corner of the hall. To the east is a raised platform. The
floor is covered with granite tiles, some are cracked and pitted. In the highday
of Moria this room was probably used as a gathering place for warriors.
name: Mustering Hall

desc:
The hall is square with a raised platform in the northern end, the floor is
covered with granite tiles, some of them cracked or stained with blood. This
place was probably used by the dwarves as a place to gather before they went to
war. Somehow the foul creatures that live in Khazad-dum seem to have adopted
this habit, except they are not going anywhere.
name: Mustering Hall

desc:
The hall is expanding to the north and the west. The outlines of a gate adorn
the eastern wall. There are no signs of a door and it appears the gate is just
that - an adornment.
name: Mustering Hall

desc:
Ornate pillars line the sides of this major road, evenly spaced along both
walls. Interspersed between the pillars are small alcoves that probably once
housed statues of honoured dwarves, but scattered alabaster fragments are all
that remain -- an unfit epitaph for these revered ancestors.
name: Mahal's Way

desc:
The wide road opens into a magnificent chamber before continuing on the other
side. The hemispherical ceiling is ablaze with bright coloured tiles, set in an
intricate mosaic pattern depicting a Dwarven rout of orcs in some battle of the
distant past. Hanging from the centre of the dome overhead is a large globe of
crystal -- still seeming to gleam palely, though whatever light it may have once
held has been long ago extinguished.
name: Vault of Glory

desc:
This great shaft once marked the informal boundary between the Dwarven city to
the east and the mines to the west. In the centre of the chimney lies a
masterpiece of dwarven craftsmanship -- a slender tower that is reputed to
stretch from the roots of the mountain to Durin's Tower at the very peak of
Zirakzigil. A great winding stairway once encircled the tower, and a multitude
of catwalks and bridges would allow one access to any of the various levels of
Khazad-dum. Most of this was destroyed shortly after Durin's Bane awoke, for the
earth was troubled and great tremors caused many of the bridges to fall away
into the inky depths. The bridge here still stands, and to the east it is
possible to proceed past the central tower and on into the city area.
name: Durin's Chimney

desc:
Mahal's Way leaves the Dwarven city to the east and arches in a great stone
bridge across Durin's Chimney on its way to the mines and the western area of
Moria. Extending from the lowest dungeons delved by the dwarves to the peak of
Zirakzigil, the pinnacle of the Silvertine, this chimney sheathes a central
tower built by the Khazad to provide access to all the levels of Moria. The
tower still stands, although most of the intricate network of catwalks and
bridges that connected it to tunnels on either side of the chasm fell prey to
the frequent spasms that shook the mountain in the time when Durin's Bane first
awoke.
name: Durin's Chimney

desc:
Durin's Chimney lies to the west, spanned by a large bridge. Large carved
columns of glossy black rock line the walls of this large road -- occasionally
connected by huge arches spanning the passage, from whence hang remnants of the
magnificent lanterns that once enlightened this black domain.
name: Mahal's Way

desc:
The broad expanse of Mahal's Way leads into a major intersection to the east.
This area has been the site of recent fighting among the tribes of orcs, as
there are several bodies bearing the markings of two distinct tribes scattered
through the hall. The fighting seems to have been most intense towards the east.
Architecturally, this area is similar to the surrounding locales, as the walls
of the road house small alcoves and are lined with pillars.
name: Mahal's Way

desc:
This major intersection was apparently the site of a recent skirmish among the
orcs. Several bodies -- some with faces caked in dried blood, others with a
pasty white substance coating arms and face -- are strewn about. All the corpses
seem to have been stripped of any usable armour and weapons. To the east and
west, one of the major roads of the dwarves of old proceeds into the blackness
-- carved runes give it the name 'Mahal's Way'. Perpendicular to this runs a
grey hall lacking any overt ornamentation.
name: The Western Hall

desc:
High overhead, lofty arches from which exotic chandeliers once hung span the
wide road. The walls feature pillars and an occasional alcove, some of which
still have fine alabaster sculptures sitting within. Apparently this area has
not been violated by the orcs as much as some other locales, for though far from
cheerful, this place does not feel unwholesome.
name: Mahal's Way

desc:
A magnificent carved arch has collapsed here -- from the lack of dust probably
not too long ago. Large blocks, with runes and carvings still visible, cover the
floor of the passage forcing one to scramble over them to continue.
name: Fallen Arch

desc:
The pillars lining the road to the west have here giving way to bas-relief
carvings. Frozen in the wall are detailed scenes from the lives of dwarves, and
even an occasional elf can be spotted among the multitude of figures. To the
east, there appears to be a chamber as the road abruptly widens in that
direction into a large open space.
name: Mahal's Way

desc:
This large circular room appears to be consecrated to Aule, the Maker, for his
likeness adorns the walls and ceiling. The exquisite relief carvings on the
ceiling depict Aule lovingly fashioning the seven fathers of the dwarves from
blocks of stone, while the murals adorning the walls depict him weeping as he
prepares to destroy them so as not to damage the designs of Eru. The floor
consists of a tiled representation of Aule, hammer in hand, standing with his
left hand on the shoulder of Durin I, first lord of Khazad-dum. There is a large
door made of stone leading to the south, while its apparent counterpart on the
northern wall appears to have been sealed permanently by crude orkish masons.
name: Vault of the Maker

desc:
A magnificent staircase, leading to the upper levels of Moria, comes to an end
here. A small plaque set into the last step bears the mark of the master Dwarven
craftsman Narvi. East and west a large hall extends, its walls lined with tiny
alcoves and large pillars. Almost unnoticed beside the grandeur of Narvi's
staircase, a small ramp going down originates at a rectangular hole in the
southern wall. The first few metres of the slope are cluttered with the remnants
of a shattered door.
name: Lore Stairs

desc:
Mahal's Way widens here to encompass two more rows of fluted columns in addition
to the pillars that line the walls. The columns are formed of snow- white marble
still unsullied by the passing centuries. The refreshing scent of outside air is
unmistakable -- it seems to originate from the east -- and combined with the
pristine marble, it gives this place the feel of a vast breezy veranda.
name: Colonnade

desc:
This chamber is walled in magnificent white marble with inset designs made of
rose-coloured quartz. The forces of nature have had a minor triumph here, for
small hallways that once lead north and south from this place are now totally
choked with piles of stone. To the west is a large hall filled with white
columns, while low steps lead up to an even larger chamber to the east. A cool
breeze issuing from the chamber to the east brings with it the promise of fresh
air and open spaces.
name: Antechamber

desc:
A glorious climax to Mahal's Way, this large chamber is lined with fine white
marble and rose-coloured quartz. Narrow pillars are spaced throughout the room,
soaring upwards and joined together by magnificent arches. High on the eastern
wall, many large shafts have been cut through the mountainside, and through them
the sky can be seen -- dispelling the gloom and despair of the black depths
under the mountain. A fresh breeze from the Dimrill Dale brings with it the
scent of pine forests, and perhaps a hint of the mallorn trees of Lothlorien
located a bit farther east.
name: The Rising Hall

desc:
Large amounts of rubbish have accumulated in this room -- everything from bits
of armour and weapons to bones with rotting bits of meat still clinging to them.
There is a large chamber of some kind to the west, while the passage south
rapidly becomes choked with rubble.
name: Refuse Room

desc:
This room contains a single bed that, although still filthy, seems luxurious
when compared to the other pallets and cots scattered through the Uruk-Moragog
environs. The ceiling here is a high dome, its original ivory colour visible
infrequently through a skin of sooty stains. At its apex, the dome has been
cracked like an eggshell, with tiny fractures radiating from a blackened area
that looks melted.
name: Cracked Dome

desc:
The heavy door to the north is still in great shape, maintained by this tribe of
orcs as a defence against incursions by whatever wanders these halls. It would
seem that a small contingent of Burzruth are stationed here, probably as a
vanguard for raids made against the orcs of the upper city levels.
name: Burzruth Guardroom

desc:
This small passage connects three large chambers. It is apparently in constant
use because the trash that characteristically clutters the unused halls of this
region is here limited to an occasional bone or morsel of meat.
name: Foul Passage

desc:
Furnished with crude tables, this room provides a common area for the black orcs
inhabiting this region. Piles of greasy furs are apparently used for lounging
about, while the flayed remains of several rats hint at other forms of
amusement. The walls are grimy, and a pall of grey smoke hangs about the upper
portion of the room. Remnants of past meals are strewn haphazardly about the
floor, and painted on the wall in child-like scrawls are repulsive scenes of
orkish debauchery.
name: Odious Chamber

desc:
A cloud of greasy smoke obscures the roof of this passage, and all the exposed
surfaces of this area are covered with an oily residue, making them slightly
slippery. The smoke originates to the west, and from that direction the scent of
charred flesh can also be detected.
name: Grimy Hallway

desc:
A large fire blazes in the centre of the room, sending clouds of greasy smoke
into the air. A large spit straddles the flame, its shaft coated with thick
deposits of blackened blood and grease. Thrown in one corner are the remnants of
three orcs -- little more than the skeletons remain as all usable parts have
been stripped and apparently devoured.
name: Fire Chamber

desc:
The floor here is tracked with grease, and wisps of oily smoke from the room to
the north curl about just below the ceiling. The masonry of the dwarves is here
almost totally obscured by the accumulated filth of centuries of use by orcs.
name: Grimy Hallway

desc:
A natural chimney has been widened by orkish masons, leaving enough room for a
fully armoured man to make his way slowly up a stair of wicked-looking iron
pitons driven into the rock.
name: Narrow Access

desc:
This plain road stands in stark contrast to the ornate thoroughfare just to the
south. The elaborate carvings and pillars common elsewhere are here replaced by
simple blocks of grey stone. A reinforced wooden door lies to the east, still in
excellent condition despite centuries of neglect.
name: The Western Hall

desc:
From the martial motif of the carvings decorating walls of this shaft, these
stairs were probably used by the Dwarven guards stationed in this area to
quickly access the levels below. The stairs are worn by heavy use, but the
carvings have been little touched by the depredations of orcs or time.
name: Mustering Stairs

desc:
This hallway, although large, is missing much of the exotic decoration found on
other grand roads throughout Moria. The walls and floor are made of large blocks
of grey rock and the ceiling hangs low. The sconces on the wall are made of
simple black iron, purely utilitarian in design.
name: Avenue of the Guard

desc:
The grey floor of this wide hall has a weathered look, as if it has seen the
passage of uncounted feet over the years. There is a large iron door leading
east, adorned with wide crimson swathes of a blood-like substance. To the north
the passage terminates at a large chamber of some sort, but there is no sign of
a door there.
name: Avenue of the Guard

desc:
The carved runes over the door suggest that in ages past, this room housed a
contingent of Dwarven guards. Small holes and notches cut into the walls are all
that remain of the racks and shelves that would hold armour and weapons, as the
wood was scavenged for other uses, or simply turned to dust over the years.
name: Northern Guardroom

desc:
This cistern supplied drinking water to the Dwarven guards stationed above. The
shallow water still here is probably very stale, but it looks drinkable. The
stone walls are very cool to the touch, and rough enough to allow one to
scramble out of this place.
name: Cistern

desc:
This room has been kept in excellent condition. It contains several crude chairs
built from scavenged wood and a few cots. The walls have been adorned in typical
Uruk-Moragog fashion, almost entirely covered with a rusty residue of dried
blood. The heavy iron door to the west is still in excellent shape, lacking even
the bloodstains that are characteristic of places inhabited by this tribe of
orcs.
name: Uruk-Moragog Guardroom

desc:
The walls and ceiling of this passage look as if they have been seared -- the
smooth stone blocks are covered with sooty stains, and some of them appear to
have been cracked by intense heat. The passage continues to the east, and opens
into a large chamber to the west.
name: Dingy Passage

desc:
The passageway turns west to south here. It is fairly clear of trash, though a
small pile of rodent bones has accumulated in one corner. To the south, a flimsy
ladder occupies the centre of the passage, coming down from somewhere above.
Overhead and on the walls, the stone is covered with large black and
ash-coloured smudges -- reminders of a large fire long ago.
name: Dingy Passage

desc:
A large stone block has been removed from the ceiling here, and a narrow tube
leading upward has been excavated. A crude ladder leads up into the hole, but it
is impossible to determine how far up it extends.
name: Foot of Crude Ladder

desc:
The walls here have a dirty appearance, as if stained by smoke, adding to the
already gloomy atmosphere. The passage is obviously well-used, for the bits of
trash and garbage so common to orc tunnels have been cleared out of the centre
by passing feet and lie only in small piles along the wall.
name: Dingy Passage

desc:
The ash-coloured passage ends here at a primitive canvas partition hung from a
flimsy wooden frame to the east. The canvas has been liberally painted with
scenes of carnage, seeming to centre around a depiction of a large orc holding
some sort of cup.
name: Dingy Passage

desc:
Filled with insect-ridden pallets, this room is obviously utilised by the orc
guards for sleeping when they are not on duty. All of the exposed stone in this
room is covered with sooty smudges, as if some time in the past a great
conflagration had burned here, staining the walls with its smoke. Apparently
there was once an exit to the south, but that has been permanently closed by the
work of orkish masons.
name: Sleeping Chamber

desc:
This small remnant of the great central stair quickly comes to an end. The
dizzying abyss below is no longer accessible by this route, and the halls of the
lower deeps must be reached by another way.
name: Precarious Stair

desc:
This corridor stands in stark contrast to the ornate roads that tunnel through
the surrounding mountain. Gone are the fluted pillars and soaring arches,
replaced by simple grey granite and occasional wrought-iron sconces, yet its
quiet decor seems somehow appropriate for the mansions of the dwarves.
name: The Western Hall

desc:
This road was designed seemingly to echo the Dwarven spirit. Its hard grey walls
seem to radiate a stoic strength drawn from the roots of mountains. All
superfluous decorations are absent, their presence would seem almost trite in
this area.
name: Avenue of the Guard

desc:
Large blocks of granite weighing several tons each have been so cunningly fit
together that the walls of this place might have been built from a single slab
of rock. The hall is silent now, though there is evidence of occasional orc
patrols -- footprints left in the thick dust.
name: Avenue of the Guard

desc:
This road was apparently designed with a spartan theme, for there are no
superfluous decorations to be seen. The heavy grey granite blocks making up the
floors and walls of this place sit in silence, seeming to wait in the darkness
for the sound of dwarves mustering for battle to echo here again.
name: Avenue of the Guard

desc:
This small space owes its existence to the caprice of chance. Large boulders are
wedged tightly into the shaft overhead, but some of the rubble to the west looks
like it may be safely moved. Below, a magnificent stairway bearing the markings
of Narvi proceeds -- apparently it used to continue through the rubble-choked
shaft above, but no longer.
name: Tiny Chamber

desc:
The majestic road of black stone that extends the west abruptly ends to the east
in a pile of rubble. The hallway here is for the most part intact, the hard
ebony rock unmarred by whatever catastrophe occurred to the east.
name: Hall of Mourning

desc:
The road continues its journey beneath the archways. The walls are intricately
carved with scenes from an ancient battlefield, with the victorious dwarves
gathered about the bodies of two slain leaders. The detail is so extraordi- nary
that the grief on each of the faces has been captured, frozen here for eternity.
name: Hall of Mourning

desc:
This area is an intersection of two large halls, crossing each other at right
angles. The grand road leading east and west is constructed of some exotic black
glassy material, while north and south, the hallway seems to possess a sanguine
glow. The pillars in this area slant inward slightly, creating an illusion of
great height.
name: Intersection

desc:
The avenue continues in a north-south fashion. The beautifully crafted walls
seem to shine with a ruddy glow undimmed by the ubiquitous filth layering all
exposed surfaces. The presence of many footprints indicate this passage still
sees heavy traffic.
name: Red Avenue

desc:
The red avenue continues south-north here, joining a smaller road passing
through a doorway a short way to the east. Grand arches carved of a reddish rock
line the avenue, their tops hidden in the darkness above.
name: Crossing in the Avenue

desc:
The avenue goes straight south-north here. The walls are solid and polished, and
where the artwork shows through, speckled bands of silver have been laid out,
crossing the low, round ceiling and joining the bands from the other wall.
name: Red Avenue

desc:
Here the red avenue bends in its course, going south and east into a smaller
chamber. The lower parts of the walls are crafted in a deep red material, while
the upper parts contain strings of various ores like silver and gold. A very
cold breeze is flowing along the floor coming from the east.
name: Bend in the Avenue

desc:
You are standing at the entrance to a tomb. Strange hollow voices seem to emerge
from the surrounding walls. They could be produced by a breeze blowing through
cavities but there is no way to be sure. To the east, a grand archway littered
with speckled bands of gold and silver marks the entrance to the tomb itself.
There is a grand sign above the doorway.
name: Entrance to the Tomb

desc:
This small chamber is the resting place of a Dwarven Lord. All over the walls
carvings depicting scenes from his life, both grand victories and defeats, can
be seen. A huge coffin stand in the middle of the room, but it has been broken
open and the bones lie scattered all over the room. Nothing is held sacred by
the foul denizens who roam these tunnels now.
name: The Tomb of Khurakzegil

desc:
There is a large hole in the floor here. A sizeable block of stone has been
uprooted from the floor of the passage, revealing a rough tube probably
excavated by orcs. A crude ladder sits in the hole, allowing one to exit in this
direction. The door to the east, though obviously a dwarven creation, is painted
with horizontal stripes of bright crimson.
name: Entrance to Barracks

desc:
The avenue continues in a north-south fashion. The walls are crafted out of a
strange deep red material combined with a shining yellow material used for the
low, round ceiling. Silvery linings cascade horizontally along the walls,
swirling here and there in intricate circles before continuing.
name: Red Avenue

desc:
The red avenue goes north-south here, joining a smaller road passing towards a
doorway to the east. Some large arches made of a red glowing material are placed
above the avenue here. There is a small crack in the ceiling between the arches,
from which some water drips down to form puddles on the ground.
name: Crossing in the Avenue

desc:
The avenue goes straight north-south here. The walls are solid and polished,
with some artwork showing in the upper parts where spiralling bands of silver
has been laid out, forming swirling patterns in the low, round ceiling. The
material in the upper parts of the walls here has given in to time and cracked
open slightly.
name: Red Avenue

desc:
Here the red avenue bends in its course, going north and east into a smaller
chamber. The lower parts of the walls are crafted in a deep red material, while
the upper parts contain strings of various ores like silver and gold. A chilly
breeze from the east is wafting along the floor.
name: Bend in the Avenue

desc:
You are standing at the entrance to a tomb. Freezing hollow voices seem to
emerge from the surrounding walls. To the east, a grand archway littered with
speckled bands of gold and silver marks the entrance to the tomb itself. There
is a large sign above the doorway.
name: Entrance to the Tomb

desc:
This small chamber is the resting place of a Dwarven Lord. Paintings depicting
scenes from his life, both grand victories and defeats, can be seen on the
walls. A huge coffin stands in the middle of the room, but it has been broken
open and the bones lie scattered all over the room.
name: The Tomb of Duraz II

desc:
Crafted of a type of hardened obsidian, this grand hallway of lustrous black
continues its east-west journey. Pillars and arches add to the high ceiling's
aura of vastness, and high up among the tops of the pillars a damp mist has
form, lazily caressing the dark stone.
name: Hall of Mourning

desc:
This large circular room lies beneath an ebony dome of jet-black rock. Dozens of
niches are cut into the circumference of the chamber; judging from the copious
amounts of alabaster fragments scattered about the statues that once filled
these niches have all been pulled down and demolished. The fountain in the
centre of the chamber is still functioning, its crystal water probably coming
straight from the melting snow atop Zirakzigil.
name: Ebony Dome

desc:
This grand hallway continues east-west here. To the east, the high ceiling
plunges downward toward the floor in an elegant arc, and the wide road passes
through the resulting portal into another chamber. The walls here are made of
the same black stone found in the surrounding areas, and the carvings present
are still as detailed as when first made.
name: Hall of Mourning

desc:
The slender column in the centre of Durin's Chimney is connected to the east
side of the chasm by a makeshift bridge of old wooden planks. A large stone
bridge once stood here -- the remains of it are clearly visible -- but it
probably collapsed during the troubled years following the awakening of Durin's
Bane, for it is said the earth would frequently shake as he made his presence
felt.
name: Durin's Chimney

desc:
The truncated remains of a mighty stone bridge precariously cling to the western
edge of this chasm. Known as Durin's Chimney, this mighty cleft in the heart of
the mountains is reputed to stretch from the deepest delvings of the dwarves to
the highest peak of Zirakzigil. Replacing the fallen bridge is a makeshift
structure of wooden planks, scarcely wide enough for two men to walk abreast. It
stretches from the western edge to the slender central pillar -- a construction
of the dwarves that once held a stairway allowing access to all levels of
Khazad-dum -- and from there to the eastern side. The column itself has been
badly damaged -- it appears that the spiralling stair that once encircled it has
been completely destroyed below this level, although it some of it does seem to
have survived above.
name: Durin's Chimney

desc:
This grand hallway obviously served an important function at one time -- a small
plaque insribed with runes labels it 'Hall of Mourning'. The cool black stone of
the floor is relatively free of dust, and the slightest sound sends faint echoes
reverberating down the length of the passage and upwards into the darkened
recesses of the vaulted ceiling. To the north there seems to be some sort of
guard station, while the hall itself runs east-west.
name: Wide Road

desc:
The stair of obsidian seamlessly melts into the glossy black rock of the floor.
Passageways extend in three directions -- a major road leads east, while to the
north and south smaller corridors lead into the murky darkness. The road leading
east was built with great care by the dwarves, for intricate bas-relief carvings
are visible along the length of its walls, until they fade from vision in the
distance.
name: Obsidian Stairs

desc:
The deep-black walls of this alley are inlaid with spiralling filaments of
shining silver. The threads seem to shine and twinkle with a light of their own
as they dance along the passageway. Scattered along both walls are the remnants
of several wicked knives, as if in their greed the denizens of this place had
tried to pry the strands from the wall.
name: Silver Lace Alley

desc:
A huge jet-black door bars the way south, though it appears to have been warped
in such a way that it will no longer close properly. The intricate silver
patterns in the wall are more prominent near the door than in areas to the
north, and they seem to centre around a carving on the eastern wall.
name: Silver Lace Alley

desc:
The walls and floor of this alley appear to be carved from a single block of
lustrous black stone -- no joints or seams are visible. Inlaid in the deep ebony
of the walls are exquisite patterns traced by strands of spun silver no thicker
than hair. They cover the wall like lace, disappearing into the vaulted darkness
overhead.
name: Silver Lace Alley

desc:
The jet-black alley continues its straight course. The intricate patterns of
inlaid silver are more prevalent here than farther south, and they dance and
swirl around a carving of Aule crafting the first dwarves in his smithy. To the
north are the remains of a shattered door.
name: Silver Lace Alley

desc:
From the scattered remnants of tools lying strewn about, it is apparent that
this room used to be an armoury. Set in the northern wall is a large forge used
by the dwarves to fashion armour and weapons in ages past. There appears to be
nothing salvageable from the scattered pieces of weaponry and armour that remain
-- everything of value must have been looted long ago.
name: Abandoned Armoury

desc:
This room is obviously used as a base for the many orc patrols that wander these
halls. Dirty pallets and cots are everywhere, and the smell of sweat and filth
makes the air almost too thick to breathe.
name: Uruk-Moragog Barracks

desc:
This small foyer for the room to the east is apparently used as a garbage pit.
Various pieces of broken armour, mostly of orkish manufacture, have been tossed
haphazardly about, and the butchered carcasses of several large rats lie
decomposing near the entrance -- skinned and stripped of meat.
name: Entrance to Barracks

desc:
You are standing in the second Uruk-Urgingurz barracks on the fourth level in
Moria. The walls are stained with dried blood, probably relics from a recent
feast on less fortunate, skinned, prey. Some black rusty shields hang on the
walls too, with a strange emblem on them.
name: Uruk Barracks

desc:
The floor to both the east and west is sunk about two metres relative to the
central section. These shallow pits once housed huge blocks of ice cut from the
snowfields of upper reaches of the mountains. Combined with the steady cool
breeze blowing from the south, this would produce a temperature cold enough to
safely store meat.
name: Ancient Icehouse

desc:
This room was perhaps another of the many rooms that once held supplies for the
Dwarven city. The roof in the northern end of the room has collapsed -- some
time ago judging by the coating of powdery dust overlaying the scene. A faint
breeze from the south occasionally forms whorls in the dirt, sending small
clouds of the stuff into the air.
name: Collapsed Storeroom

desc:
An ancient mouldering skeleton lies prostrate on the floor, the upper portion of
its cranium completely smashed as if by a huge club. At one time, this skeleton
may have belonged to a human -- the teeth indicate it was not an orc-- but
whoever it was died hundreds of years ago, judging by the layers of dirt that
have covered many of the bones.
name: Service Corridor

desc:
This corridor probably saw little use even when the dwarves inhabited Moria, for
the whole area is lined with simple bricks -- there is no sign of the splendid
decor seen in the more important passages of Khazad-dum. The hall continues to
the east, unmarked by superfluous decoration, while to the west the masonry
seems to have suffered damage at some time in the past.
name: Service Corridor

desc:
This room was once much larger -- the entire area to the west apparently
collapsed many years ago. A thick layer of powdery dust coats everything,
kicking up in great clouds when disturbed. The ceiling here looks extremely
unstable, as great fractures zig-zag across its entire area. Passages to the
south and east are still free of rubble, allowing one to move in either of those
directions.
name: Storage Room

desc:
This room has been devastated at some time in the past. The walls to the east
and west have cracked, revealing the natural rock lying behind the old masonry.
Large blocks of granite have fallen from above, and the ceiling is riddled with
fractures. The dust lies thickly here, undisturbed except for scattered rat
tracks and droppings.
name: Storage Area

desc:
This room has been cleverly hidden from prying eyes. Pegs adorn the walls for
the hanging of armour and several weapon racks for any weapons. Obviously from
the layout of this small hall, the equipment here was carefully stored away,
ready for a unit of dwarves in need of armament. The peg in the middle must hold
the armour of the Lieutenant.
name: Old Dwarven Armoury

desc:
Several shattered chests indicate that this room may have once housed some of
the fabled treasures of Moria, but there is nothing of value visible among all
of the debris. The room is made from the same black rock that lines the hall-
way to the north, and the wall to the south is covered in bas-relief carvings
decorated with inlaid silver.
name: Plundered Treasury

desc:
This is a small niche in the wall, most likely used as a guardpost. There is a
very uncomfortably looking chair made of stone here, but one can assume by the
look of it and its position in the room most of the guards on duty here prefer
to stand as they watch the passing creatures. This niche would also make a good
ambush spot.
name: A Niche

desc:
The carvings on the wall seem to indicate that this room was at one time used by
the dwarves for ceremonial purposes, but now this place has been pirated by the
Uruk-Goramog for a dwelling place. Smelly rags and furs are strewn haphazardly
about, and a crude wooden table carries the remnant of a recent repast.
name: Northern Guardroom

desc:
This room is utilized as a crude dining hall by the orcs of this area, a con-
This room is probably utilized as a crude dining hall by the orcs of this area,
a conclusion supported by the presence of a large pile of bones stashed in one
corner. The artwork adorning this room -- depicting Aule taking a dwarven king
close to his bosom -- suggests that this room may have had a ceremonial purpose
originally.
name: Southern Guardroom

desc:
The magnificent stairway comes to an abrupt halt here. The continuation below
has been entirely choked with rubble -- the beautiful craftsmanship of Narvi
totally destroyed by orcs. To the west a large passage lined with pompous arches
and pillars extends, while to the south is a large door of solid iron.
name: Lore Stairs

desc:
In this room some Uruk warriors have their quarters. Broken pieces of armour and
weapons are scattered over the filthy floor, and contraptions which probably are
used as beds stand in a row along the eastern wall. In the corners of the room
you see piles of rotten meat, probably relics of a mid-night snack.
name: Lower Barracks

desc:
A wide road passes in the west-east direction. Obviously one of this level's
most used roads, it is brilliantly crafted with pillars lining up along the
walls. Small busts of renowned dwarves are placed on cutouts in the walls. The
deep-black floor is apparently made of a material of great hardness since it is
in very good shape after all these years without maintenance, though there do
appear to be a few scratches in the area near the northern wall.
name: Wide Road

desc:
This narrow tunnel is has been little touched by the orcs -- except for a small
section connecting with the room to the east, this place is essentially a
natural chute. The floor is tilted at a steep angle down to the east, and it
appears that it is possible to continue down through a hole by the eastern wall.
name: Steep Tunnel

desc:
This natural tube has been lined with crude pitons of black iron, allowing one
to proceed with care both up and down. There is a steady stream of oily smoke
issuing from the darkness below -- it is being steadily drawn upward.
name: Natural Chimney

desc:
Here the avenue makes a surprising bend west, going up some fairly broad and low
stairs. There are tracks in the stairs, suggesting that they were used for
escalating wagons of some kind. As is the case with most of this avenue, this
part is not very fashionably painted or designed, but it was probably not meant
to be used by the whole population. The finish on the panels on the northern
wall has cracked open, revealing the underlying rock.
name: Base of Low Stairs

desc:
The avenue is going north-south here. Part of the roof has fallen apart and
there are some boulders spread across the floor, contributing to the overall
decayed look of this avenue. It is too bad the present inhabitants apparently do
not care enough for aesthetics to do some serious repairs around here.
name: Decayed Avenue

desc:
This is the beginning of a quite wide passage apparently used for service
purposes, since it is not very ingeniously crafted. Plain marble panels line the
walls, without any particular paintings on them. Some tracks in the floor are
hardly noticeable, suggesting that wagons of kinds were rolled here when this
place was inhabited by dwarves.
name: Decayed Avenue

desc:
The narrow stairway continues spiralling about the narrow pillar, desperately
clinging to the masonry and refusing to break free and plunge into the dark-
ness below. Looking down, there seems to be a landing of some sort, while in the
gloom above, the underside of a mighty stone bridge can be discerned.
name: Remnant of Central Stair

desc:
The shoddy cots and grimy piles of fur scattered about indicate that this
chamber is used for sleeping by the vanguard of Burzruth stationed in this level
of Moria. This chamber may have had a ceremonial use once because the orcs have
not managed to destroy completely what must have once been a multitude of
intricately carved runes on the walls, though for the most part they are
unreadable now.
name: Sleeping Chamber

desc:
An extremely unpleasant odour dominates this section of passage -- the odour
becomes distinctly more pronounced to the east. The grey granite blocks of the
floor and walls are covered with smudges, and several tracks that appear to be
made of some type of horrible smelling mud lead into the chamber to the west.
name: Malodorous Passage

desc:
The horrid smell issuing from the room to the north conjures visions of open
sewers choked with filth. The dark smudges of footprints covering the floor
indicate that whatever area lies to the north is often visited by the denizens
of this place.
name: Malodorous Passage

desc:
This room has been appropriated by the orcs for use as a sewer. Whatever prior
function this area had, it is readily apparent that nothing wholesome can live
here now. The stench of urine and faeces is overwhelming, like a thick wall
preventing entry into the room.
name: Chamber of Feculence

desc:
Stony rubble prevents any progress to the south. From the layers of dust coating
everything, this passage appears to have been unused for quite a long time. A
few dusty cobwebs hang from the ceiling, gently swaying as if caught in an
imperceptible breeze.
name: Choked Passage

desc:
The golden vein ends here. A large hall opens up to the north paved with granite
stone and looking very martial. A small set of stairs heads down to a lower
level.
name: Golden Road

desc:
These stairs, connecting the magnificent Golden Road with the fifth level of
Moria, are made of a combination of gold and naurond. The two materials
intertwine each other and spiral around the stairs adding to the majestic
atmosphere of the level above you.
name: Ore Stairs
